title: सन्तोषी माता आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सन्तोषी माता आरती ॥

जय सन्तोषी माता जय सन्तोषी माता।
अपने सेवक जन की सुख सम्पत्ति दाता॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

सुन्दर चीर सुनहरा माँ का रूप निराला।
सिर पर मुकुट विराजत तिलक लाल अति भाला॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

गणपति की हैं दुलारी शुभ सन्तोष मनाती।
जो मन से माँ को ध्यावें सबका दुख मिटाती॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

गुड़ और चना है प्यारा माँ भोग लगाती।
शुक्रवार का व्रत कर माँ खुश हो जाती॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

निर्धन को धन देती माँ सन्तान भी देती।
जो कोई शरण में आवे बिगड़ी बात बनाती॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

पतिव्रता को सदा तू रखती लाज बचाये।
गरीबों की सुनती माता दुख सब मिटाये॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

सोलह शुक्रवार जो माँ का व्रत करते।
मनवाँछित वर पाते सब दुख संकट टरते॥

जय सन्तोषी माता...

माँ सन्तोषी की आरती जो कोई नर गावे।
ऋद्धि सिद्धि सुख सम्पत्ति सब कुछ पावे॥

जय सन्तोषी माता जय सन्तोषी माता।
अपने सेवक जन की सुख सम्पत्ति दाता॥

॥ इति श्री सन्तोषी माता आरती सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Santoshi Mata Aarti ||

Jai Santoshi Mata Jai Santoshi Mata.
Apne Sevak Jan Ki Sukh Sampatti Data.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Sundar Chir Sunehra Maa Ka Rup Nirala.
Sir Par Mukut Virajat Tilak Lal Ati Bhala.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Ganpati Ki Hain Dulari Shubh Santosh Manati.
Jo Man Se Maa Ko Dhyaven Sabka Dukh Mitati.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Gud Aur Chana Hai Pyara Maa Bhog Lagati.
Shukravar Ka Vrat Kar Maa Khush Ho Jati.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Nirdhan Ko Dhan Deti Maa Santan Bhi Deti.
Jo Koi Sharan Mein Aave Bigdi Baat Banati.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Pativrata Ko Sada Tu Rakhti Laaj Bachaye.
Garibon Ki Sunti Mata Dukh Sab Mitaye.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Solah Shukravar Jo Maa Ka Vrat Karte.
Manvanchhit Var Pate Sab Dukh Sankat Tarte.

Jai Santoshi Mata...

Maa Santoshi Ki Aarti Jo Koi Nar Gave.
Riddhi Siddhi Sukh Sampatti Sab Kuchh Pave.

Jai Santoshi Mata Jai Santoshi Mata.
Apne Sevak Jan Ki Sukh Sampatti Data.

|| Iti Shri Santoshi Mata Aarti Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
जय सन्तोषी माता! आप अपने सेवकों को सुख-सम्पत्ति देने वाली हैं। आपका रूप सुन्दर, सुनहरा और निराला है, सिर पर मुकुट विराजता है और ललाट पर लाल तिलक है।

आप गणपति की दुलारी हैं, शुभ सन्तोष मनाती हैं। जो मन से माँ का ध्यान करता है, उसका दुख मिटता है। गुड़ और चना माँ को प्रिय भोग है, शुक्रवार का व्रत करने से माँ प्रसन्न होती हैं।

निर्धन को धन देती हैं, सन्तान देती हैं, शरण में आने वालों की बिगड़ी बात बनाती हैं। सोलह शुक्रवार व्रत करने से मनवाँछित वर मिलता है।

जो इस आरती को गाता है, उसे ऋद्धि-सिद्धि, सुख-सम्पत्ति सब कुछ प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Essential aarti for Santoshi Mata Friday worship
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and grants material abundance
Invokes the blessing of children and family happiness
Protects chaste women and preserves their honor
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of wishes when sung with devotion on fridays
description: The Santoshi Mata Aarti, known in Sanskrit as सन्तोषी माता आरती, is a traditional worship song (aarti) in praise of Goddess Durga. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to fulfill all wishes when sung with devotion on Fridays as well as protect chaste women and preserves their honor. Additionally, it is known to bestow children and family happiness. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Santoshi Mata Vrat for enhanced blessings. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Santoshi Mata Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Santoshi Mata Aarti in Hindi with transliteration. Popular Friday aarti for Santoshi Mata worship for prosperity and family blessings.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Santoshi Mata and how did her worship spread?
seoFaqs.answer: Santoshi Mata is the Goddess of Satisfaction and Contentment, regarded in popular tradition as the daughter of Lord Ganesha and granddaughter of Shiva-Parvati. Her worship was relatively localised until the 1975 Hindi film 'Jai Santoshi Maa' brought her into mainstream pan-India bhakti. The Friday vrat tradition and the aarti however predate the film and trace to North Indian household devotion.

seoFaqs.question: What is the 16 Fridays (Solah Shukravar) Santoshi Mata vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: Devotees observe a vrat for 16 consecutive Fridays. Each Friday they bathe early, wear clean clothes (preferably red or yellow), eat only one meal without sour foods, place a Santoshi Mata image and a kalash with gud (jaggery) and chana (chickpeas), recite the Vrat Katha and Aarti, and distribute the gud-chana as prasad. After 16 weeks an udyapan ceremony is performed feeding eight young boys.

seoFaqs.question: Why are gud and chana the standard offerings?
seoFaqs.answer: Jaggery (gud) and roasted chickpeas (chana) are simple, sattvic, easily-available foods that emphasise Santoshi Mata's principle of contentment with little. The strict prohibition on sour foods (lemon, tamarind, curd, pickle) on her vrat day comes from the Vrat Katha — sourness symbolises bitterness and complaint, the opposite of santosh (satisfaction). The prasad must be shared, never eaten alone.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning and benefit of Santoshi Mata Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The aarti 'Jai Santoshi Mata, Maiya Jai Santoshi Mata' invokes the Mother as the giver of sukh-sampati (happiness and prosperity) to her devotees. Traditional benefits promised include marital harmony, return of estranged family members, financial relief, healing of chronic ailments, and most importantly an attitude of contentment that transforms suffering. It is sung at the close of every Friday vrat puja.

seoFaqs.question: Are there any rules to follow on Santoshi Mata vrat day?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — strict avoidance of sour or acidic foods (khatta) by the vrat-keeper and ideally the entire household on Fridays during the 16-week vrat; only one meal of sattvic food; no anger or harsh words (matching her name santosh); offerings of gud, chana and yellow flowers; distribution of prasad to children especially. Breaking the sour-food rule is said to nullify the vrat and is dramatised in the Vrat Katha.
